text.title: Technical Debt Trade-off Worked Example
text.summary: Worked example: inline auth check shortcut classified as deliberate-prudent with an "endpoints >= 3" repayment trigger.

section.title: Scenario
section.p: Architect shipping Q2 Stripe webhook integration. Auth check was inlined in /orders handler instead of being extracted to a FastAPI dependency. Better solution: extract to dependency injector (3 days). Backlog is at 14.2% debt of 20% budget.

section.title: Input
Output — debt-record
section.code: { "debt_id": "DEBT-0014", "title": "Inline auth check in /orders POST; not extracted to middleware", "intent": "deliberate", "prudence": "prudent", "severity": "localized", "code_area": ["api/orders.py"], "shortcut": "Auth check inlined in the handler to ship the Stripe webhook integration in time for the Q2 launch.", "better_solution": "Extract to FastAPI dependency injector; covers /orders + 6 future endpoints uniformly. ~3 days work.", "repayment_trigger": { "metric": "endpoints_requiring_same_auth_check", "operator": ">=", "threshold": 3, "source": "git grep + endpoint count in api/routers/" }, "budget_cost_pct": 0.6, "current_total_debt_pct": 14.2, "linked_adr": "ADR-0018" }
